name: Site Development & Grading
summary: Site development, land, and public-realm infrastructure engineering design-side work performed remotely.

faqs.q: Do you provide on-site surveying?
faqs.a: No, field data is supplied by the client or their local surveyor.

name: Site / Civil Engineering Design
summary: Comprehensive civil engineering design for development projects.

faqs.q: Do you offer stamped deliverables?
faqs.a: Yes, stamped deliverables are available through our licensed PE partner network covering all 50 states.

name: Stormwater Management & Drainage Design
summary: Design of stormwater management systems to meet local compliance and environmental goals.

faqs.q: Are your designs compliant with local codes?
faqs.a: Yes, we design to the codes specified and provide stamping via our PE network.

name: Civil Permitting & Entitlement Support
summary: Drawing preparation and support for civil permitting and entitlements.

faqs.q: Do you submit the permits directly?
faqs.a: We prepare the drawings and support documentation for the client or local agent to submit.

name: Structural Building Design
summary: Structural design for new construction and additions across various building types.

faqs.q: Do you provide stamped structural drawings?
faqs.a: Yes, through our network of licensed Professional Engineers covering all 50 states.

name: Structural Steel Detailing
summary: Precision steel detailing for fabrication and erection.

faqs.q: What software do you use for detailing?
faqs.a: We utilize industry-standard 3D modeling and detailing software (e.g., Tekla Structures, Revit).

name: Rebar Detailing
summary: Detailed reinforcement drawings and bar bending schedules.

faqs.q: Do you detail to specific regional codes?
faqs.a: Yes, we detail according to ACI, CRSI, or other specified regional standards.

name: Precast & Concrete Detailing
summary: Comprehensive detailing for precast and concrete structures.

faqs.q: Can you coordinate with factory requirements?
faqs.a: Yes, we tailor our shop drawings to match specific precaster manufacturing processes.

name: Seismic Analysis & Retrofit Design
summary: Seismic evaluation and retrofit design for existing structures.

faqs.q: Do you perform on-site inspections?
faqs.a: No, we rely on client-supplied inspection or survey data for our analysis.

name: Structural Condition Assessment
summary: Analysis of structural condition based on provided field data.

faqs.q: How do you assess without visiting the site?
faqs.a: We work from client-supplied inspection data, laser scans, and existing drawings.

name: Architectural Design & Development
summary: Design-phase architecture from concept through design development (SD/DD).

faqs.q: Can you take over a design mid-phase?
faqs.a: Yes, we frequently support firms by taking schematic designs into design development.

name: Construction Document Sets
summary: Preparation of comprehensive construction document (CD) sets.

faqs.q: Are your CD sets ready for permit?
faqs.a: Yes, they are prepared for permit submission, and stamped via our PE/Architect partner network if required.

name: Interior Design
summary: Space planning, programming, and interior architectural design.

faqs.q: Do you handle furniture selection?
faqs.a: We handle FF&E (Furniture, Fixtures, & Equipment) coordination and schedules as requested.

name: 3D Rendering & Visualization
summary: High-quality 3D renderings and visualizations for presentations and marketing.

faqs.q: What do you need to start rendering?
faqs.a: We can work from sketches, CAD files, or Revit models.

name: Façade Design & Shop Drawings
summary: Detailed design and shop drawings for building envelopes and façades.

faqs.q: Do you coordinate with structural requirements?
faqs.a: Yes, façade design is heavily coordinated with our structural engineering models.

name: Sustainable Design & LEED Consulting
summary: Design strategies and consulting to achieve sustainability goals like LEED certification.

faqs.q: Do you guarantee LEED certification?
faqs.a: We provide the design and documentation support necessary for the certification process.

name: Mechanical (HVAC) Engineering
summary: Mechanical and HVAC system design for optimal building comfort and efficiency.

faqs.q: Do you perform load calculations?
faqs.a: Yes, we perform comprehensive heating and cooling load calculations.

name: Electrical Engineering
summary: Electrical engineering including power, lighting, and low-voltage systems.

faqs.q: Does this include low-voltage systems?
faqs.a: Yes, we design data, telecom, security, and fire alarm layouts.

name: Plumbing Engineering
summary: Design of domestic water, sanitary, and specialized plumbing systems.

faqs.q: Do you design medical gas systems?
faqs.a: Yes, we design specialized systems for healthcare facilities.

name: Fire Protection Engineering
summary: Fire protection and life safety system design.

faqs.q: Are your designs NFPA compliant?
faqs.a: Yes, our designs are based on NFPA and local code requirements.

name: MEP Coordination & Clash Detection
summary: Multi-trade coordination across mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and fire protection systems.

faqs.q: What phase should coordination start?
faqs.a: Ideally at Design Development, but no later than early Construction Documents.

name: MEP Shop & Installation Drawings
summary: Detailed installation and shop drawings for MEP contractors.

faqs.q: Do you provide fabrication-ready drawings?
faqs.a: Yes, we provide drawings suitable for prefabrication and on-site installation.

name: Industrial Facility Design
summary: Facility engineering and planning for industrial and manufacturing clients.

faqs.q: Can you design for specialized environments?
faqs.a: Yes, we design for cleanrooms, hazardous environments, and heavy manufacturing.

name: Process & Piping Design
summary: Design and modeling of complex process piping systems.

faqs.q: Do you provide stress analysis?
faqs.a: Yes, pipe stress analysis can be included depending on project scope.

name: Equipment Layout & Plant Design
summary: Spatial planning and layout of manufacturing equipment and plant infrastructure.

faqs.q: Do you coordinate with vendor equipment models?
faqs.a: Yes, we integrate vendor-supplied 3D models into the master plant model.

name: Manufacturing & Warehouse Engineering Support
summary: Engineering support for material handling, warehouse logistics, and manufacturing upgrades.

faqs.q: Can you help optimize an existing facility?
faqs.a: Yes, we often provide layout options to improve workflow and safety.

name: 3D BIM Modeling
summary: Architectural, Structural, and MEP 3D BIM modeling from LOD 100 to 500.

faqs.q: What LOD do you usually work to?
faqs.a: We typically model to LOD 300 for design and LOD 400 for construction/fabrication.

name: Scan to BIM
summary: Conversion of point cloud data into accurate 3D Revit models.

faqs.q: Do you capture the point cloud data?
faqs.a: No, we convert point cloud data provided by the client or their surveyor.

name: BIM Coordination & Clash Detection
summary: Cross-disciplinary clash detection and resolution to prevent field issues.

faqs.q: What software is used for coordination?
faqs.a: We typically use Navisworks Manage or BIM Collaborate Pro.

name: Revit Family Creation
summary: Creation of parametric and non-parametric Revit families for manufacturers and design firms.

faqs.q: Are the families optimized for file size?
faqs.a: Yes, we build lightweight families that don't bloat project models.

name: 4D Scheduling & 5D Cost Estimating
summary: Integration of project schedules (4D) and cost data (5D) into the BIM model.

faqs.q: Do you provide the schedule?
faqs.a: We link the client's schedule (e.g., Primavera P6, MS Project) to the model elements.
